summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a blues‚Äëheavy Ann‚ÄØArbor show with standout Sampson‚ÜíCumberland and a memorable Terrapin‚ÜíDrums, though the set feels short and low on improvisation.
review: The sbd‚Äëmatrix source is praised for its crystal‚Äëclear mix and clean instrument separation, making this a very listenable document of the 1989 Ann‚ÄØArbor concert.  The performance leans heavily into blues numbers ‚Äì US Blues, Sampson, Cumberland and China¬ÝDoll ‚Äì with several reviewers highlighting the surprise US‚ÄØBlues close and the Sampson‚ÜíCumberland transition as high points, while noting a relatively short second set and limited jamming overall.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently rated excellent (‚âà4.5‚Äë5‚òÖ) while show quality is described as decent but not spectacular (‚âà3.0‚Äë3.5‚òÖ). Weighted average (70% show, 30% recording) yields ~3.8‚òÖ.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly call the sound "great", "killer" and "crisp"; the matrix blend is described as clean with good instrument balance and no major drop‚Äëouts.

The concert leans heavily on blues cuts (five in total) and lacks extended improvisation.
